en.extract:
The austral blackbird (Curaeus curaeus) is a species of bird in the family Icteridae. It is found in Argentina and Chile, and it migrates to regions close by. Its natural habitats are temperate forests, subtropical or tropical high-altitude shrubland, and heavily degraded former forest. 


Description
Its plumage is entirely black with a slight bluish hint, and its physical features consist of black legs, black eyes, and a sharp beak with missing pigmentation in the head, chest, and wings. Compared to other birds in its family, the Austral Blackbird has a highly varied voice, and they do a lot of singing and calling together. 


Habitat
Their main habitats are woodlands, forests, lakeshores, beaches, fields, valley bottoms, and rocky shores. It is commonly found in small flocks of 6 to 20 in and around the forest, and never in the open land. Can be commonly found in protected areas in national parks around Chile and Argentina. They are found in protected areas where the conditions support proper living and breeding. 


Diet
They are typically drawn to nectar and like feeding on different kinds of flowers. When the bird feeds on these plants it will create a yellow color from the nectar around their head and face that changes its look to the viewer's eye. They are considered omnivores and like feeding on seeds such as oats and wheat. They also like to eat wild fruits and regular fruits such as cherries and grapes. 


Population & Migration
Its population size and trend have remained stable, and the bird is not endangered as they are considered Least Concern with no current threats to their species. Its migration patterns are largely resident and they migrate to west-central Argentina, and on the Atlantic coast of Patagonia. Their exact population size is unknown but is around under 10,000. 


Breeding
The breeding season for the Austral Blackbird takes place between the months of October and December, and it is done in their nests made from plants and mud. They have nests located in Argentina with incubated eggs, and recently hatched chicks in mid-December. They only prefer to nest in habitats on hills usually found in trees in the forest and not open urban areas. They also have nests in Chile with 3 to 6 eggs made out of twigs, grass stems, bamboo leaves, and mud.

The '''austral blackbird''' ('''''Curaeus curaeus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Icteridae]]. It is found in [[Argentina]] and [[Chile]], and it migrates to regions close by. Its natural [[habitat]]s are temperate [[forest]]s, subtropical or tropical high-altitude [[shrubland]], and heavily degraded former forest. 

== Description ==
Its [[plumage]] is entirely black with a slight bluish hint, and its physical features consist of black legs, black eyes, and a sharp beak with missing [[Pigment|pigmentation]] in the head, chest, and wings.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last1=Fraga |first1=Rosendo |last2=Pantoja-Maggi |first2=Vicente |date=2024 |title=Austral Blackbird (Curaeus curaeus), version 1.1 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/ausbla1/cur/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.ausbla1.01.1 |issn=2771-3105|url-access=subscription }}</ref> Compared to other birds in its family, the Austral Blackbird has a highly varied voice, and they do a lot of [[singing]] and calling together.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Austral Blackbird |url=https://ebird.org/species/ausbla1}}</ref> 

== Habitat ==
Their main [[Habitat|habitats]] are woodlands, forests, lakeshores, [[Beach|beaches]], fields, [[Valley|valley bottoms]], and [[Rocky shore|rocky shores]].<ref name=":0" /> It is commonly found in small [[Flock (birds)|flocks]] of 6 to 20 in and around the forest, and never in the open land.<ref name=":0" /> Can be commonly found in protected areas in [[National park|national parks]] around Chile and Argentina.<ref name=":0" /> They are found in protected areas where the conditions support proper living and breeding.<ref name=":0" /> 

== Diet ==
They are typically drawn to [[nectar]] and like feeding on different kinds of [[Flower|flowers]].<ref name=":1" /> When the bird feeds on these plants it will create a yellow color from the nectar around their head and face that changes its look to the viewer's eye.<ref name=":1" /> They are considered [[Omnivore|omnivores]] and like feeding on [[Seed|seeds]] such as oats and wheat.<ref name=":0" /> They also like to eat wild [[Fruit|fruits]] and regular fruits such as cherries and grapes.<ref name=":0" /> 

== Population & Migration ==
Its population size and trend have remained stable, and the bird is not [[Endangered species|endangered]] as they are considered Least Concern with no current threats to their [[species]].<ref name=":2">{{Cite report |url=https://www.iucnredlist.org/species/22724271/263302102 |title=Curaeus curaeus: BirdLife International: The IUCN Red List of Threatened Species 2024: e.T22724271A263302102 |last=IUCN |date=2024-06-12 |publisher=International Union for Conservation of Nature |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2024-2.RLTS.T22724271A263302102.en |language=en}}</ref> Its [[Animal migration|migration patterns]] are largely resident and they migrate to west-central Argentina, and on the Atlantic coast of [[Patagonia]].<ref name=":0" /> Their exact [[Population (biology)|population]] size is unknown but is around under 10,000.<ref name=":2" /> 

== Breeding ==
The [[breeding season]] for the Austral Blackbird takes place between the months of [[October]] and [[December]], and it is done in their [[Nest|nests]] made from plants and [[mud]].<ref name=":0" /> They have nests located in Argentina with incubated [[Eggshell|eggs]], and recently hatched chicks in mid-December.<ref name=":0" /> They only prefer to nest in habitats on hills usually found in [[Tree|trees]] in the forest and not open [[Urban area|urban]] areas.<ref name=":0" /> They also have nests in Chile with 3 to 6 eggs made out of [[Twig|twigs]], grass stems, bamboo leaves, and mud.<ref name=":0" />

zh.extract:
南美黑鹂（学名：Curaeus curaeus），是雀形目拟鹂科的一种鸟类，属于单型的南美黑鹂属（学名：Curaeus）。
南美黑鹂体重约83.7克，翼长约12.45厘米，喙宽度约6.8毫米，喙厚度约9.7毫米，嘴峰长约31.8毫米，跗蹠长约33.8毫米，尾长约10.20厘米。
南美黑鹂具有部分的迁徙性，栖息在森林，它们是食肉动物。


亚种
Curaeus curaeus curaeus，分布在阿根廷南部和智利南部，以及麦哲伦海峡。
Curaeus curaeus recurvirostris，分布在智利麦哲伦省。
Curaeus curaeus reynoldsi，分布在火地群岛、纳瓦里诺岛和奥斯特岛。
